terminal.comment: What you get when you hire us

Manifesto.heading:
The doubts clients have about outsourcing —
addressed up front.

items.title: Fast response, fast start
items.desc: Reply within 1 business day. Once the quote is confirmed, work starts in ~3 days on average.

items.title: Code built for handover
items.desc: TypeScript · consistent conventions · README + operations guide. Any developer can pick it up without friction.

items.title: We finish what we start
items.desc: Domain, deployment, and monitoring set up by us. 1 month of free maintenance after delivery.

items.title: Fast decisions, tight timelines
items.desc: Planning, design, dev, and deploy under one roof — less communication overhead, schedules that hold.

Services.heading: What kind of site do you need?
Services.subheading: Six common project types we ship. Happy to discuss anything else — just ask.

items.title: Landing page
items.desc: Product launches, campaigns, hiring pages, and more.

items.title: Company / brand site
items.desc: About, portfolio, careers — multi-page sites with depth.

items.title: E-commerce
items.desc: Payments, inventory, and order management — ready to run yourself.

items.title: Mobile invitations
items.desc: Weddings, events, and other invitation pages — mobile-first.

items.title: Site refresh
items.desc: Migrations, redesigns, and feature additions for existing sites.

Marquee.footnote: — We use standard tools so clients can run things themselves later.

Pricing.heading: Rough pricing.
Pricing.subheading: Real quotes depend on page count, features, and design scope. The numbers below are starting points — we'll send an exact quote when you apply.
Pricing.footnote: All prices exclude VAT. Domain, third-party APIs, and paid fonts are billed separately.

Portfolio.sectionSubheading: Live projects and case studies by category. Tap a card to see details.

items.title: Team branding site
items.summary: Colorful single-page site that captures the team's identity.
items.description:
A single-page site introducing the team and their activities.
Member colors are visualized as gradient badges, conveying the team's diversity. Deployed as a fast static site on Vercel.
items.headline: Built around the team's identity.

items.title: Internal ops admin
items.summary: An admin web app that runs the whole ops dashboard from one screen.
items.description:
Users, schedules, work logs, meeting notes, approvals, expenses — all the data ops needs in one place.
Includes a preview mode you can browse without login, plus role-based access control, fast search, and snappy routing.
items.headline: One screen for the whole operation.

items.title: Korean-style e-commerce demo
items.summary: A Korean-style e-commerce demo with a Coupang/Musinsa feel.
items.description:
An e-commerce demo built around patterns familiar to Korean shoppers.
It ships with category/best/new/deal routing, product detail, a cart drawer, and search — all the flow of a production-ready store.
items.headline: From discovery to checkout, in one flow.

items.title: Fine-dining restaurant site
items.summary: A classic restaurant site in the spirit of Eleven Madison Park.
items.description:
Large serif type and restrained photography set the mood for a fine-dining site.
Includes menu, reservations, private events, gift cards, and a journal — everything an operating restaurant needs.
items.headline: A reservation, set in serif.

items.title: General hospital portal
items.summary: A general-hospital portal modeled after Asan Medical Center.
items.description:
A demo motivated by the information density and trust-building tone of Korean general hospital portals.
Ships with 6 main subpages (about · departments · doctors · checkup · reservation · support) and a 4-step booking form ready to run.
items.headline: Care, organised at a glance.

items.title: Mobile wedding invitation
items.summary: A mobile wedding invitation in the Mocheong/KakaoTalk style.
items.description:
A mobile wedding invitation that fits in a single scroll.
Full-bleed hero, D-day countdown, lightbox gallery, account accordion, location, and guestbook — every flow you'd actually use.
items.headline: Their day, in everyone's pocket.

PriceEstimator.subheading: Pick a project type and rough size. Real quotes come after we talk — this is just a starting point.

Process.heading:
From inquiry to delivery —
in 4 clean steps.
Process.subheading: 2–4 weeks on average. We tell you exactly what you'll receive at each step.

steps.title: Inquiry
steps.desc: Send your project details. We reply within 1 business day.

steps.title: Scope & contract
steps.desc: Once scope, features, and timeline are confirmed — contract sent, 50% upfront.

steps.title: Design & build
steps.desc: Figma review, then build. Weekly staging previews so you see progress.

steps.title: Launch & handover
steps.desc: Domain, deployment, and ops guide. Final 50% on delivery.

FAQ.heading: Frequently asked questions.
FAQ.subheading: Not finding what you need? Add it to the application form — we'll cover the rest in our intro call.

items.q: How long does a project take?
items.a: Landing pages 1–2 weeks, company sites 2–3 weeks, e-commerce/web apps 3+ weeks depending on scope. You'll get an exact timeline with the first reply.
Based on page count, features, and design scope. You'll get an initial quote within 1 business day of submitting the form, then a final quote and contract after we align on scope.

items.q: How many design review rounds are included?
items.a: Standard includes 2 review rounds. Additional rounds are billed per round. Reasonable revisions within scope are no charge.

items.q: Who owns the code and rights?
items.a: Full source code and copyright transfer to the client after final payment. GitHub repo ownership is transferred along with it.

items.q: Do you handle domain and hosting?
items.a: Yes — domain purchase, plus Vercel/Cloudflare/AWS setup. We recommend keeping these accounts in your name so you control the recurring costs.
1 month of free bug fixes after delivery. After that, monthly retainers or per-issue support are both available.
50% upfront, 50% on delivery. Tax invoices available. Projects over USD 2,000 can be split into 3 payments by agreement.

items.q: Can you redesign an existing site?
items.a: Yes. We review the existing code first and decide together whether migration or a fresh build is the right path.

items.quote: I was worried at first, but decisions were fast and the polish was there.

items.quote: Pulled the essentials together in a single planning meeting — kept everything on schedule.

items.quote: Payments and account integrations were handled end-to-end — almost no work for ops at handover.

items.quote: Site feels noticeably faster after the redesign. SEO score climbed too.

items.quote: One person for design + dev cut our communication cost in half. Meetings halved.

items.quote: Small post-launch tweaks got fast turnaround. Trust grew — we hired again.

items.quote: The internal admin is genuinely usable. Our ops team picked it up day one.

items.quote: First time outsourcing — clear progress at each step kept me at ease the whole way.

CTA.heading:
Ready to
start your project?
CTA.description: Fill out the form below — we'll reply within 1 business day. Specs not finalized? That's fine. We'll work through them with you in the intro call.

page.heading: Tell us about your project.
page.subheading: We reply within one business day. Light questions are welcome — feel free to send a draft.

steps.title: Submit the form
steps.desc: Fill out the form. Takes about 5 minutes.

steps.title: First review
steps.desc: We'll send an initial quote and timeline within 1 business day.

steps.title: Kickoff
steps.desc: We align on details, then work begins.

steps.title: NDA-friendly
steps.desc: Your data is securely deleted 1 year after the project closes.

page.footerCta: Send a casual note to sales@efface.dev instead.

form.submitNote: We'll reply to your email within 1 business day.

placeholders.name: Your name
placeholders.description: What kind of site do you need? Reference sites, key features, scope — anything helps. (min. 20 characters)

attachments.hint: Wireframes · references · brand guides / up to {max} files, 5MB each

form.agree: (required) I consent to the collection and use of my personal data (name, email, phone) for the purpose of processing this inquiry. Data is retained for 1 year after closure.
